name: Frank Li
education: Philosophy & Psychology from University of Toronto; Computer Science & Software Engineering from Western University
headline: Software Engineer at Microsoft
about:
I enjoy working in teams rich in diverse skillsets to solve difficult problems. I have a passion for developing a deep understanding of the client, planning and designing novel elegant solutions, and coming together to create software that enables and empowers those who rely on it.

I also have an continuously learning mindset where I spend an couple hours everyday before work sharpening my skills and learning new technologies. I find though that I learn the most when I'm mentoring others so if there is something you feel you can learn from me please feel free to reach out. 

My areas of expertise
AI
Specialized in leveraging AI, GPT, and ML 
Experience working as director of AI

Frontend
React, React Native,  Native Android development, Javascript, GraphQL, Cypress, Xamarin

Backend 
Java, GoLang, C++, C#, Dot Net, SQL, Docker, Postgres, Nginx, Rust, Golang

Business
Logistics and Delivery
Developer Tools
E-discovery and technology in law
Adaptive learning
Wellbeing Productivity Software

currentPositions.description:
Glint is a cutting-edge people success platform designed to elevate organizational performance by enhancing employee engagement and fostering personal development. Utilizing a combination of social science, machine learning, natural language processing, and advanced language models, our platform empowers businesses to achieve outstanding results by unlocking the full potential of their workforce.

Frontend: Angular, Typescript, Redux 
Backend: Java, NodeJS Microservices, Rust
Cloud: AWS, ECS, SQS, RDS, Elastic Search, Cognitive Search, 516108 ts

experiences.title: Software Engineer (VIVA Glint)
experiences.jobDescription:
Glint is a cutting-edge people success platform designed to elevate organizational performance by enhancing employee engagement and fostering personal development. Utilizing a combination of social science, machine learning, natural language processing, and advanced language models, our platform empowers businesses to achieve outstanding results by unlocking the full potential of their workforce.

Frontend: Angular, Typescript, Redux 
Backend: Java, NodeJS Microservices, Rust
Cloud: AWS, ECS, SQS, RDS, Elastic Search, Cognitive Search, 516108 ts

experiences.title: Software Engineer (VIVA Insights)
experiences.jobDescription:
Modern Workplace Transformation. Making jobs easier and lives better. In a time of unprecedented worldwide challenge and change, the work we're doing on the Modern Workplace Transformation team has never been more important. Our priority is empowering the nearly two billion frontline workers around the globe. We're having a positive effect on the world at a time it really needs it.

- Building platform for context specific nudges detecting customer's current actions and context to respond with helpful insights in a performant and smooth manner.
- Leading AI feature team in leveraging AI and GPT technology to build products that help executives to manage their organization and deal with urgent situations. 
- Investigating and solving difficult distributed system problems and improving the robustness and reliability of our product.

Frontend: JavaScript, TypeScript, React
Backend: Substrate, Cosmos DB, C++, C#
Cloud: Azure, Azure Bot, Azure Resource Group, Azure Key Vault

experiences.title: Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
- Winning award for the engineer of the quarter given to the best engineer who joined within the quarter
- Taking on responsibilities of frontend team lead overseeing and planning frontend architecture
- Mentoring and teaching junior software engineers on best practices
- Raising the bar of operational excellence on the codebase. Moving the team over to Typescript and Cypress testing.
- Working closely with the product manager and designer to understand requirements.
- Building a complex on-road monitoring product used by dispatchers around the world to coordinate and plan the logistics of delivery. 
- The app is launched in 15 countries in North America, Europe, and the East Asian region.
- Implementing and preparing product launch in Saudi Arabia, Egypt, Belgium, Turkey, and the Netherlands. Adding in 
proper localization and right to left support.
- Adding driver breaks feature within Cortex promotes proper work-life balance for delivery drivers.
- Investigating and solving complex distributed system problems and bringing team together around solution.

Frontend: JavaScript, React, Microfrontend, Typescript, GraphQL
Backend: Java, Lamdas, DynamoDB, SQS, Kinesis (Flink), Elastic Search, SNS, SQS, CodeSuite, Rust

experiences.title: Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Tread is a digital platform that makes the business of moving construction materials easier, faster and more profitable.

Frontend: Javascript, React, Flow Typing, GraphQL
- Working closely with fellow developers focusing on frontend, designers, and product managers to create fast, elegant 
looking user experiences.
- Spent time speaking with different clients and help build empathy and offer insight to understand our users. Meeting with 
clients on various features and planning. 
- Gave an engineers perspective on solutions to pain points. Working with designers on team to workshop ideas to improve features and maximize positive impact.
- Adding Cypress end to end testing to frontend development workflow. 

 - Calling a meeting with all frontend developers to collaborate and commit to proper testing moving forward. 

 - Adding tests to buildkite and setting up CI to test all Github branches before allowing merge.
- Coordinating and organizing meetings for frontend developers and designers to agree on important metrics that make a 
large impact for clients. Assigned each metric to a developer so that there is an inhouse domain expert that can track these metrics over time. Metrics include update responsiveness, client's devices personas, optimistic updates, and speed indexes.

Backend: GoLang, SQL, GOQU, 
- Creating, managing, and maintaining docker images. Improved the efficiency of production and dev environments by 
creating docker layers for catching between builds.
- Creating graphQL queries, resolvers and SQL queries. 
- Pushing to production for weekly builds while monitoring buildkite and AWS. 
- Using and building with the use of Goqu for cleaner object-oriented code.

Team: Agile/Scrum/Sprint
- Took on responsibility of running sprint poker and sprint grooming to plan out and estimate sprints. 
- Added input on retrospectives after each sprint and collaborated with other team members on how we can improve and 
become more effective.

experiences.title: Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
iCONECT Development, LLC is an industry leader in developing innovative legal review software and services that empower legal teams to complete complex review projects more cost-effectively.

Frontend stack: Javascript, ReactJS, ASP.NET MVC, Typescript, HTML5/CSS3, jQuery
Backend stack: C++, C#, and MySQL

•	Maintaining and building new features on the various existing web and mobile applications
•	Designing software architecture and building new web applications and mobile applications
•	Scaling, maintaining, and improving various existing codebases and systems  infrastructures
•	Maintaing and improving the development and QA environments of various applications
•	Contributing to the ongoing improvement of engineering practices and procedures
•	Incorporating modern technologies, including ReactJS, ASP.NET MVC, Typescript, HTML5/CSS3, jQuery, C++, and MySQL
•	Working in an Agile environment, delivering features quickly and iteratively with the team.
* Working with API development team to develop modular tests which ensure good client experience as well as proper secruity. This also includes  testing OAuth 2 by creating a in house third party app.
* Built frontend components which integrates iConect machine learning features allowing for training, testing, and execution of AI assisted ediscovery.
•	Worked in Xamarin to develop IOS and Android apps for police evidence collection.

experiences.title: Founder & Software Engineer
experiences.jobDescription:
Avocadocore is an adaptive learning platform that helps students learn by providing instant and constant feedback. It also provides tools for educators to build their own unique questions and provides helpful analytics to tell them which students are struggling and requires extra help. 

Frontend stack: Javascript, React, React Native, Flux, Typescript
Backend stack: Python, Flask, MySQL,  Java


Our main software AVO has been deployed and is being used in multiple classes in Western University. We work with professors and students to continue improving our software's capabilities to help students learn.

• Full Stack Developer for a startup company with 2000 unique visitors per month and a team of 10 developers.
• Working with professors as clients to develop learning software desired.
• Built software from ground up setting up Flask server and interactive platform built in React and React Native. Using JS, C, Python and running an integrated Full Stack solution
• Interviewing and selecting applicants to add into the company and deciding based on their behavior and skills which department they would work fit best in. Mentored new members until they are in sync with the workflow and culture at the company
• Coordinating cooperation between different departments and their team leads i.e. Server Team, Frontend Web Team, Mobile Team, and Math Code Team. Leading in efforts to improve documentation & code skills sharing across teams.
• Proactively seeking out gaps in technical understanding, especially cross-functional, and works with a variety of teammates to fill the gaps. Regularly influencing multiple teams to align around a cross-team goal.

experiences.title: Program Associate for Humanitarian Issues Promotion
experiences.jobDescription: Lead youth ambassadors in outreaching as many communities as possible through youth outreach to community centers and events. Coordinate Youth Red Cross conferences, perform public speeches to represent the Canadian Red Cross, run workshops, and assist with encouraging volunteer activism.

updates.postText:
I recently received Amazon's Engineer of the Quarter award.

I wanted to say thank you to my teammates and manager for their help and support!

skills.title: U S. Department of Defense Compliance

volunteerAndAwards.description:
I love software development because of the potential for each person to create technical solutions that empower those around them. Gitlab is one such open-source software that has empowered me and supported my previous startup Avocadocore. 

I am here to contribute and give back to help other young entrepreneurs succeed. For contributions see "My Projects" section below.

recommendationsReceived.name: Zal Machado
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Film Producer, Former Senior Software Engineer
recommendationsReceived.context: Zal worked with Frank on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description: I worked with Frank as a fellow software developer at Tread. He showed great initiative, and helped set up our integration test suite almost as soon as he started working at the company. He was always willing to ask questions and help in working out a problem anyone on the team had. He cares deeply about front end architecture and process and brought a lot of value to the product, as well as helping to implement practices that have helped the team succeed in the long term. He is a great addition to any team that would have him!

recommendationsReceived.name: Zhan Hong Liau
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Engineering Lead
recommendationsReceived.context: Zhan Hong was senior to Frank but didn't manage Frank directly
recommendationsReceived.description: Frank was an absolute joy to work with. He's always inquisitive, willing to take the initiative on things he sees as necessary, and approaches everything with a great attitude. With his time at Tread he has made positive contributions to our processes and documentation, alongside his work in evolving our product. His energy was felt not only in the engineering team, but organization wide. I look forward to working with him again in the future!

recommendationsReceived.name: Justin White
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Product Designer
recommendationsReceived.context: Justin worked with Frank but on different teams
recommendationsReceived.description:
While at Tread, Frank and I worked closely on a new set of payments features. He brought a wonderful energy to the team and he was very easy to collaborate with. We'd often bounce ideas off one another about anything from the overall feature experience all the way down to component level best practices. I really appreciated working with Frank because of his interest and engagement with design.

Frank is a curious, driven and very positive all around team player – I would highly recommend him.

recommendationsReceived.name: Natalie Ronson
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Software Developer
recommendationsReceived.context: Natalie worked with Frank on the same team
recommendationsReceived.description:
I worked with Frank on the Payments team at Tread. As well as being a strong developer, Frank was a great team member! He was always willing to collaborate and figure out a problem. He also initiated a lot of discussions around front end best practices, javascript tips and testing, which helped us all share our knowledge and ideas.

Frank brings a lot of positive energy to the table and I would highly recommend working with him!

recommendationsReceived.name: Philip Nicolcev
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Software Developer - DevOps
recommendationsReceived.description: Frank is a pleasure to work with. He brings a very positive vibe to his teams and is always eager to learn and volunteer for new tasks. He was a valued member on our react and golang project team and showed skill when using our tech stack. I'd recommend him for anyone looking for a JS or golang developer who works well with others on a team.

recommendationsReceived.name: Mary Papagiannis
recommendationsReceived.subtitle: Senior Talent Manager @ ZayZoon | Founder @ Campfire || Named one of The Peak’s 2023 Emerging Leaders + Flexa’s 2024 Future of Work Pioneers || Recruiter | Startup Advisor | Public Speaker
recommendationsReceived.context: Mary worked with Frank but on different teams
recommendationsReceived.description: Frank's energy and warmth are incomparable. He is always looking for ways to improve processes, bring people together, and consistently has thoughtful touchpoints with people across the organization. He is amazing at recognizing others' contributions and highlighting their strong suits, lifting those around him. Frank is incredibly driven, smart, and would be an asset to any organization. I can't wait to see what he accomplishes!

recommendations.name: Moe Als
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Product Designer | UX/UI Design, Research | AI, Enterprise Software, SaaS, B2B/B2C
recommendations.context: Frank worked with Moe on the same team
recommendations.description:
Moe has been a standout senior designer on our team, working across various projects and with multiple clients. His creative vision and meticulous attention to detail have significantly elevated the quality and impact of our designs. Moe's ability to translate complex concepts into visually compelling designs is unparalleled, and it consistently impresses our clients. He’s not just about making things look good—he ensures that every design element works harmoniously to meet strategic objectives.

Beyond his technical skills, Moe's collaborative approach and leadership have made him a pillar of our team. He mentors junior designers, fostering an environment of learning and innovation. His proactive communication keeps projects flowing smoothly and ensures that all stakeholders are aligned with the vision and progress. Clients frequently praise Moe for his professionalism and the effectiveness of his designs, making him a trusted and invaluable asset to our team.

recommendations.name: Mohammed Kaleem
recommendations.subtitle: IT Systems Support Officer - Program Area Manager || Justice Technology Services - Justice Video Network || Ministry of Solicitor General || Government of Ontario || Ontario Public Service
recommendations.context: Frank worked with Mohammed but on different teams
recommendations.description: Mohammad helped me with receiving my onboarding equipment when I first joined Microsoft. He was patient, helpful, and responded to my questions quickly. I very much appreciate his professionalism and would highly reccomended him.

recommendations.name: Zal Machado
recommendations.subtitle: Film Producer, Former Senior Software Engineer
recommendations.context: Frank worked with Zal on the same team
recommendations.description:
Zal and I worked together on the dispatching product team at Tread. During my time working here I would often go to Zal about his opinion and advice on frontend architecture. I also found that I was always able to learn something new from his code reviews which made me a better programmer. 

For what it's worth I highly reccomend Zal!

recommendations.name: Laura Liggett
recommendations.subtitle: Customer Support Specialist @ Mediafly
recommendations.context: Frank worked with Laura on the same team
recommendations.description:
I worked with Laura while I was a software engineer at Tread. 

As someone who was completely new to the construction industry, I started off with very little understanding of the clients and their needs. Laura worked with me to provide a holistic picture of the customers I am building software for which helped me build not only understanding but empathy for the client's needs. Laura is great at summarizing and explaining complex problems in a way that drives positive action. 

For what it's worth I highly recommend Laura!

recommendations.name: Zhan Hong Liau
recommendations.subtitle: Engineering Lead
recommendations.context: Zhan Hong was senior to Frank but didn't manage Frank directly
recommendations.description:
I worked with Zhan while I was a software engineer at Tread. 

Zhan was never directly on the same team as me, but he might as well have been given all the mentorship and advice he gave me. When I get tasked with the more complex features or challenging problems beyond my experience the first senior engineer I turn to is Zhan. He always took the time to weigh in with his expertise which contributed to more reliable and efficient features. 

I really appreciate all the ways working under Zhan's guidance has helped me grow as an engineer. For what it is worth I highly recommend Zhan!

recommendations.name: Philip Nicolcev
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Software Developer - DevOps
recommendations.context: Philip was senior to Frank but didn't manage Frank directly
recommendations.description:
I was a software engineer working under Phil's leadership at Tread. I learned a ton about Golang backends, docker, and AWS deployments from him. I especially appreciate how he always takes the time to teach the team the systems he builds and how they work. 

For what it's worth I highly recommend Phil!

recommendations.name: Justin White
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Product Designer
recommendations.description:
As a software engineer, I highly recommend Justin as a colleague. I worked with Justin at Tread focusing on billing and payments for Tread's clients. This was an important feature to drivers, many of whom are immigrants trying to make ends meet, who rely on Tread's ease of use and accuracy to know how much they are being paid for their work and when. The companies who are paying drivers also depended on this feature for their budgeting and projections.

As an engineer who focused on frontend work, I always felt excited to work on bringing Justin's designs to light because it always feels intuitive and feels clean. I also especially appreciate that Justin is always open to questions and feedback. We would spend hours going back and forth making sure a new feature feels and behaves in the most optimized way for our clients. 

I feel like I learned a lot working with Justin and we created some awesome things. For what it's worth I highly recommend Justin!

recommendations.name: Oluwasegun Modupe
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Process Engineer | Ph.D. Chemical Engineer | Biopharma & Manufacturing Expert | Tech Transfer | GMP | Data-Driven Problem Solver
recommendations.description: I taught Oluwasegun guitar while at the University of Toronto. Oluwasegan was a great student who learned quickly and asked engaging questions. A pleasure to work with him and I wish him success!

recommendations.name: Mary Papagiannis
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Talent Manager @ ZayZoon | Founder @ Campfire || Named one of The Peak’s 2023 Emerging Leaders + Flexa’s 2024 Future of Work Pioneers || Recruiter | Startup Advisor | Public Speaker
recommendations.context: Mary was senior to Frank but didn't manage Frank directly
recommendations.description:
As a software engineer I think Mary is one of best Heads of People you could have helping you perform at your best. I joined Tread during the 2020 Covid pandemic and despite working completely remotely I've never felt more engaged in my team's collective mission. I think this was in large part thanks to Mary's great work.

Mary was the head of people at Tread when I applied for their software engineering role. Through my interviews with Mary her kindness, consideration, and thoughtful questions made me even more excited at the prospect of working at Tread. 

During my time working at Tread I always felt like she is someone I can approach with any question or problem. The social events she organized really helped me get closer to my team and overall made me more effective in my role. I also especially appreciate the little things Mary did to bring the team together whether it's hand written notes to or small holloween treat boxes sent to everyone's address. 

For what it's worth I highly recommend Mary!

recommendations.name: Danielle DeHart
recommendations.subtitle: Executive Assistant to SVP, Chief Technology & Innovation Officer
recommendations.description:
Danielle is by far one of my favorite recruiters to work with. She is incredibly helpful and encouraging and goes out of her way to help me find a fitting software engineer position which matches my experience and goals.

Working with her has given me a positive first impression of Amazon AWS and made me want to be a part of the organization that much more. 

For what it's worth I highly reccomend Danielle.

recommendations.name: Stephanie Brown
recommendations.subtitle: Helping construction leaders pressure-test their technology choices on real projects
recommendations.context: Stephanie was senior to Frank but didn't manage Frank directly
recommendations.description:
I really appreciate the things I learned working with Stephanie. 

When I first joined Tread as a software engineer I had very little background and understanding in construction. This gap makes it more difficult for me to understand and empathize with clients as I work with the team to build great software. 

From the first day I joined Stephanie gave me the context and background for the industry and for those we are helping through Tread's software. I feel that every coffee chat with her gave me greater insight which ultimately led to better decision making when building out features.

recommendations.name: Charlotte T.
recommendations.subtitle: Senior Web Developer
recommendations.context: Frank worked with Charlotte but they were at different companies
recommendations.description:
Charlotte is patient, careful, and considerate when it comes to her work. She is someone who is extremely attentive to detail and reliable. Best of all I really like the positive energy and good vibes she brings to each team she is on. 

I highly reccomend Charlotte!

recommendations.name: Brandon Dely
recommendations.subtitle: Software Engineer | Building High-Performance, Scalable, User-Centered Products
recommendations.context: Brandon was senior to Frank but didn't manage Frank directly
recommendations.description:
Brandon taught a ton of things which I think will always be useful for me as a dev. I learned how to navigate a large codebase and find bugs from him. He also taught me how to work with iView and even stayed behind after work to help me when I was really stuck.

I really appreciated having him as a mentor and a colleague!

recommendations.name: Loewe Ke
recommendations.subtitle: Parallel Entrepreneur | Innovator | Advisor | Management Consulting, AI, Tech, Data | Tedx Speaker | F.I.R.E @ 29 | Serve & Maximize Human Values
recommendations.context: Frank worked with Loewe but they were at different companies
recommendations.description: Loewe is a great mentor and friend who has given me great advice and feedback for my startup Avocadocore. Really appreciate the invaluable experience he has offered me. He is a great person to work with and I highly recommend him!

recommendations.name: Julie Winer
recommendations.subtitle: Software Developer
recommendations.context: Frank worked with Julie on the same team
recommendations.description: Julie is a wonderful person to work with and an invaluable resrouce to Avocadocore's team. She is someone who naturally takes lead and empowers those working with her to excel.

peopleAlsoViewed.headline: Software Engineer | Building High-Performance, Scalable, User-Centered Products
Software Engineer @ Microsoft                                  University of Toronto Alumni

summary: Frank Li is currently a software engineer at Microsoft and the founder of Avocadocore, an early-stage adaptive learning startup, combining large-tech experience with hands-on startup leadership. He frequently posts about software engineering (with emphasis on backend and full-stack development), AI and robotics (including experiments with ChatGPT), adaptive learning and edtech, career support and referrals, and industry news such as construction technology. His posting style is approachable and community-oriented—mixing personal reflections, shout-outs to colleagues, curated news links, and practical career advice rather than deep technical treatises. Frank conveys practical technical credibility (system design notes, product launches, and awards) alongside empathy and networking energy: he amplifies hiring needs, celebrates teammates, and actively asks his network to help displaced engineers. Notable patterns include sharing educational resources, promoting team and product achievements, cross-posting relevant AI/industry research, and mobilizing his network for referrals and opportunities. His unique value lies in bridging big-tech credibility (Microsoft/Amazon recognition) with founder-level product experience in edtech and a demonstrated role as a connector and advocate in his professional community.
highlights: Awarded Engineer (Amazon Engineer of the Quarter)
